title: Development of simple and sensitive spectrophotometric method for the detection of Abacavir sulphate in bulk and dosage forms using Azocarmamine-G
author: T. Manikya Sastry
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, G. V. P. College of Engineering, Visakhapatnam-530 041, Andhra Pradesh, India 

  E-mail  : tmsastry@yahoo.com 

 Department of Chemistry, GITAM University, Visakhapatnam-530 045, Andhra Pradesh, India 

 Department of Basic Sciences and Humanities, Vignan’s Institute of Information Technology, Duvvada, 

 Visakhapatnam-530 049, Andhra Pradesh, India 

  Manuscript received online 16 August 2013, accepted 11 December 2013  

 A simple and sensitive visible spectrophotometric method has been developed for assay of Abacavir sulphate (AVS), an antiviral agent, in bulk and dosage forms. It is based on the formation of ion-association complex involving the secondary amino group of Abacavir sulphate and the acidic dye Azocarmamine-G (λ max  = 540) to form colored species. All the variables have been optimized and are validated statistically. Beer’s law limits are found in the range of 50–150 µg mL –1 . Recovery is in the range of 99.38 ± 0.87 to 99.97.2 ± 0.92%. The proposed method is simple, economical and sensitive for the quantitative determination of Abacavir sulfate.
